summary: Weapon and shot-feedback budget instrumentation is wired; entry-width changes add no assets, shared effect-pose math is lightweight, and Playwright production smoke remains green, but visible FPS still needs production profiling.
observations: Three selected GLB weapon models plus their shared texture total 164055B of source payload.
Debug asset metrics now include weapon loadedAssetIds and assetLoadErrors.
Manual browser smoke at 844 x 390 observed about 57 FPS with VAULT active.
Latest 844 x 390 smoke observed about 56 FPS with SPARK active after shot feedback was added.
Coordinate HUD is DOM text updated on the existing 250ms HUD cadence.
assetBuild query rewriting changes public asset URLs but does not add GPU resources.
Playwright browser smoke passed all five viewport gates with serial workers.
qaCapture=1 enables preserveDrawingBuffer for QA readback only.
Weapon-aware movement adds a manifest-derived footprint probe.
WeaponSystem wall avoidance adds one close-wall ray/probe path during update.
The level pinch fix removed six walkable cells and added no render payload.
The structural entry-width fix opened eight walkable cells and added no render payload.
weaponViewPose.ts adds camera-local shot-effect math and a rounded debug effectPose field.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport gates after the entry-width and shot-effect alignment changes.
The production JS chunk reported by Vite is about 642.83 kB minified and still triggers the known large chunk warning.
decisions: Preload the three initial weapon GLBs because the payload is small and weapon switching should be instant.
Use npm run validate:assets to catch missing files, size drift, and hash drift.
Do a production-preview FPS comparison before importing heavier enemy or building assets.
Keep shot collision as tile raycast until enemies/projectiles justify a broader collision system.
Keep coordinate reporting in the lightweight DOM debug HUD.
Use Playwright for correctness smoke, not as the final performance benchmark while qaCapture is enabled.
Keep the weapon clearance probes for now because they are map-sample based and low cost.
Keep shared shot-effect pose math unit-testable rather than adding more scene graph probes.
caughtIssues: Detached loaded weapon models need explicit disposal when the weapon system is torn down.
Initial weapon asset package missed the shared GLB texture dependency.
Dev-server smoke did not show a locked 60 FPS.
Cache-busted URLs can create separate browser-cache entries across builds.
Vite still reports the large chunk warning for the Three.js-heavy bundle.
Weapon avoidance is approximate and should be profiled if close-wall checks expand beyond map samples.
Vite's large chunk warning still needs a future code-splitting review before the asset roster grows.
risks: Future larger weapon sets should not all preload by default without level/load-group gating.
The current 57 FPS dev smoke needs a production-preview comparison before increasing scene complexity.
Future cache analysis should compare build ids to avoid mistaking intentional URL changes for duplicate asset leaks.

jsonForAgents.weaponClearanceCost: one movement footprint probe plus one weapon wall ray/probe
jsonForAgents.shotEffectPoseCost: shot-time and short-lived visible-effect camera-local vector math
